insight: The user needs the tool to take over at least parts of his task reliably so that he has the biggest benefit of using the tool.
quotes:
Perspektive/Effizienz: Die „Wunschvorstellung“ ist, dass man wissenschaftliche Mitarbeiter „jedenfalls in Teilen sparen“ kann, wenn Agenten die Recherche auf ähnlichem Niveau übernehmen.

„Das ist wie wenn man auf den Pool von wissenschaftlichen Mitarbeitern zugreift. Da kann man mal Glück haben und manchmal hat man Pech … und wenn man richtig Pech hat, dann kriegt man … einen renitenten wissenschaftlichen Mitarbeiter…“

insight: The user needs the answers to be precise and on point so that his time is efficiently used.

insight: The user needs the tool to be transparent if it does not have any sources available.

Primary sources are missing: Judgments were cited almost exclusively via secondary sources.
Formularhandbücher used as a substitute source: unsuitable for doctrinal questions
The citation system should differentiate between primary and secondary sources.
The user needs a complete list of the sources additionally to synthesized results.
The user wants a consistent citation format with Pin Cites.

The users want the tool to integrate into existing systems for file storage.
A prompt library as a central working resource
Clear guidance options for controlled AI deployment (similar to a data room approach)
Stronger linking of references in pleadings or contracts
A follow-up question function implemented within the answer output window.
The option to compare different documents uploaded within a matrix.
The functions of the matrix are self-explanatory, and the displays are well-structured.
status: Well done - positive feedback outweighs negative

insight: The user needs the tool to be able to process also scanned documents.

insight: The user needs to be able to export the entire chat, including all sub-questions.
userGroup: Small Law Firm; Big Law Firm; Small Law Firm; Corporate Law Department
quotes: "I want to download externally but reload easily, with reasoning included" (Corporate Personas)

insight: An option to set the context window size to 250,000 or higher would be desirable.
Implement VL capabilities in order to also be able to use floor plans for rental agreements.
The users want to be able to have the reasoning directly at the changed clause in order to have full transparency for themselves and others.
The user needs the AI to seamlessly integrate into word by inserting and prompting directly in the word environment without additional manual effort on their end.
userGroup: Mixed; Corporate Law Department; Corporate Law Department
quotes:
“Ideally, I would like to have the entire workflow in a sidebar: select a clause, have it checked, receive a proposed change, view sources, and jump directly from the sidebar to the Beck database without any media discontinuity.”
"It would be great if you could attach it to an email or paste it in" (Corporate Personas)

insight: The user wants to be able to see the sources of the generated content directly in word in order to have full transparency.
The users want to be able to mark sections within a word document and prompt referring only the selected content.
The user expects the tool to directly use the words tracked changes feature, so that they themselves keep the overview of the changes and can send it to other parties.

The users expect by using the word add in that the formatting templates they use and the formatting that the document has is obeyed by the AI when changes and insertions are made, so that they don't have to fix formatting.
The users expect the word-add-in to be intuitive and offer guidance so that they don't have to watch tutorials to be able to use it.
quotes: Said that colleagues already have „enough difficulty introducing AI into their life, which shouldn't be made extra hard by giving them extra effort to learn".

insight: The users expect the research functionality to be available through the word interface within the word-plug-in, in order to avoid switching touchpoints if they want to research something while working on a document.
status: Well done - positive feedback outweighs negative

insight: The user expects each change suggestion to come with a reasoning, why it should be changed in this way, in order to make an informed decision on the change.
The user expects change suggestions to come with the supporting law of the reason for the change, to see which source it was derived from.
The user needs to be able to get an overview of all changes to evaluate them in context, to be able to make a decision on each of the changes.
The user needs to be able to preview changes before they are applied in the document in order to feel in control and be able to individually review them.
quotes: “I find it good that I can look at it separately first and then... So that nothing accidentally gets in or I overlook something.”

insight: The users wish to be able to have transparency of why changes have been made even after inserting them into the document in order to allow themselves or another party to have full transparency into reasoning.
The user would like to be able to apply all changes after reviewing them, in order to not have to approve each one individually.
The user needs the tool to be able to offer automatic insertion or preview of changes for manual approval in order to be able to work efficiently in all cases.
quotes: “For me it depends on the quality of the changes. If its high, then an auto modus would be better. But if I have to revert all changes and make further changes anyways, I think I would prefer to preview.”

insight: The user needs to be able to lock parts of a document so that they are not edited by the AI anymore.
The user would like to be able to tell the tool how harsh or lenient the AI should be when reviewing and suggesting changes to a document.
The user expects the tool to adjust the drafting to individual style and preferences.
notes: Personlization as holistic topic should be looked into.

insight: The user needs to be able disable tracking changes so that they can quickly iterate from scratch until they have a solid state from which they then want to track the essentiell changes being made.
quotes: “So at the moment when I’m creating a draft [from scratch], it might actually not be so clever to have all changes directly always in Track Changes mode, because creation often has a different speed there.”

insight: The user wants to know which file is affected if one of the files can't be read.
The user expects a clear distinction between thinking process and content output in order to navigate the result.
userGroup: Big Law Firm; Corporate Law Department; Publisher

insight: The user need the document drafting to be more detailed in order to be able to continue working with the generated document.
The user would like to be supported in drafting answers to a request in understandable language.
status: Well done - positive feedback outweighs negative

insight: User wish for a seamless integration of all features within the tool (e.g. Workflows, matrix, Chat).
userGroup: Big Law Firm; Big Law Firm; Mixed; Corporate Law Department; Mixed

insight: User would like to have an admin view where they can see metrics of usage behaviour in their company in order to target trainings and licenses accordingly,
The user needs the tool to use further external data sources.
userGroup: Big Law Firm; Mixed; Corporate Law Department; Prosecution/ Judges

insight: The user needs a possibility to connect the individual steps of a workflow  with each other or with decision points.
The user wants a tool case that already involves example e.g. prompts / steps which they can reuse or adjust to their needs.
The user wants to be able to add prompts to  a tool case which they can reuse or adjust to their needs.
The user would like to be able to assign specific documents to a workflow/ step within a workflow.
The user would like to be able to create workflows in cooperation with the tool  by e.g. prompting an intial workflow that is set up by the tool.
The user would like to be able to improve  an existing workflow  in cooperation with noxtua by  active prompting.
The user would like to be able to improve  an existing workflow  in cooperation with noxtua by e.g. getting recommendations.
The user needs to be able to create decision points for their workflows.
The user needs a dashboard or a whiteboard on which I can insert single prompts which all can be integrated into one workflow.
The user needs to be able to (re-)name the individual workflows.
The user would like to be able to define triggers, which would lead to starting the workflow.
The user would like to have the option to set the trigger for the workflow to manual.
The user would like to be able to create/ adjust a workflow using drag & drop to make it es effortless as possible.
The user would like to predefine in the workflow which specific parts of a template should be adjusted/ filled based on the input.
The user would like to get suggestions for new workflows based on his usage of the AI.
The user would like the AI to suggest next logical steps based on the already existing ones in a workflow.
The user would like to stay in control and decide whether they want suggestions for workflow improvements from the AI or not.
The user would like to only provide the input for the workflow and then to have it run autonomously.
The user needs to be able to retrace the results of  every step of the workflow, in order to double check results.
The user needs to be able to check used sources in a workflow easily (i.e. one click).
The user would like to be actively involved if multiple options to proceed arise.
The user would like to be able to adjust an existing workflow based on their needs (e.g. changing parameters).
The user would like to be able to also run parts of workflows only.
The user needs to be able to share workflows with other users individually.
The user needs to be able to share workflows/ templates with certain user groups (e.g. department, legal area).
The user wants to be able to provide access to workflows not only for individual workflows but also by workflow groups, that they can set up .
The user wants other users to only be able to use the workflow not edit it, so that their workflow is not ruined.
The user needs to be able to collaborate within workflows, i.e. not only one person can give input/ review steps in the workflow.
quotes: "If multiple participants could input into the workflow, that's a universal tool" (Corporate Personas)

insight: The user needs to be able to export the final result that a workflow produced for documentation/ sharing  purposes.
The user needs to be able to use his own documents (e.g. files, folders) within workflows.
The user would like to be able to use matrices in workflows.
The user would like the workflows to be able to use the drafting tools (i.e. canvas, word-add-in) for text creation.
The user wants to be able to store and use word documents within a knowledge base.
The user wants to be able to store and use PDF documents within a knowledge base.
The user wants to be able to store and use Excel documents within a knowledge base.
The user wants to be able to store and use Email documents within a knowledge base.
The user wants to be able to store and use Powerpoint documents within a knowledge base.
The user wants to be able to store and use format templates from a knowledge base.
The users would like to be able to use old files, that can be accessed to draft documents.

insight: The user wants to be able to store and use pleadings in a knowledge base.
The user wants to be able to store and use rulings/ judgements in a knowledge base.
The user wants to be able to store and use internal guidelines in a knowledge base.
The user wants to be able to store and use additional legal commentary in a knowledge base.
The user wants to be able to store and use additional external data sources (e.g. laws from other countries) in a knowledge base.
The user wants to be able to manually select documents from the knowledge base for a prompt.
The user needs the solution to work really well to be able to let the solution choose relevant documents from within the knowledge base on its own for a case.
The user needs to be able to get an overview of existing documents within the knowledge base.
The user needs documents within the knowledge base to have meta data available to them.
The user needs to create folders within the knowledge base in order to be able to structure their documents.
The user needs to be able to share files in the knowledge base with individual users.
The user needs to be able to share files in the knowledge base with ceratin  teams.
The user needs to be able to share files in the knowledge base with all users.
The user needs also people without license to be able to input documents into a knowledge base as input material.
The user needs content from the documents within the knowledge base to be cited precisely word by word.
The user needs content from the documents within the knowledge base to be used only as reference (not exact matching of phrasing)
The user needs to be able to search through documents within the knowledge base.
The user needs to be able to manually add and edit meta information of a file stored in the knowledge base.
The user wants to be able to retrace from which document a information orginiated from.
The user wants to be able to add tags in the knowledge base to improve filtering and searchability.
The user wants to be able to ask questions to documents within their knowledge base in order to identify the answer.
The user wants the AI to automatically update  the information in the knowledge base based on changes in the law or commentaries.

The user needs the knowledge base and especially editing and uploading of documents to be retricted in order to make sure information is correct and not everybody can change documents or upload them publicly without review.
The user wants to be able to seleting whole folders  for usage in a prompt in order to not have select all documents manually.

insight: The user would like to enable clients to take over parts of their work supported by AI, so that they are more the reviewing and strategic instance.
The user needs the tool to support me in documenting my approach and the usage of AI, in order to make sure that I don't run into liability issues.
userGroup: Big Law Firm; Corporate Law Department
quotes: "Internally nothing for a while, then everything appears at once. Supervisors want to see what we considered, excluded, included - I can use the reasoning to document that" (Corporate Personas)

insight: The user needs to be able to give access to their files on a case-by-case basis in order to ensure control.
The user needs the tool to adjust the output to the purpose that I wanna use it for (e.g. if The user wants to use it in an email, the language and lenght should be adequate).
quotes: "Sometimes I need a quick answer; you could go more into the concrete work situation" (Judiciary Personas)

insight: The user needs the tool to provide them with practical usage examples and explains it step-by-step to me so that I can learn how to best use the tool.
The user needs a digital "Schmierzettel" in order to organize my thoughts and findings to put it together later.

The user needs to be able to restrict certain features (such as file or outlook integration) for certain user groups, that work on highly confidential topics or are too junior to judge what can be shared.
The user needs to be always the one controlling where information is send by the tool (colleagues, clients etc.).
status: Well done - positive feedback outweighs negative

insight: The user needs the AI to help me find uncertainties or potential errors by evaluating its own performance/ certainty.
The user needs the tool to monitor sources (newsletters, databases etc.) and provide me with an overview of recent changes and what they imply regularly.
The user needs a quick overview of what the tool can and can not do, in order to start working with it and learning on the go.
The user needs the tool to support using gender neutral/ inclusive speech or at least give me the opportunity to enable it.
The user needs the tool to be able to work with documents in different languages.
The user needs the tool to be approved by the "Anwältekammer" in order to be able to trust it with confidential information.
The user needs the producers of the AI to commit to ethical training of the AI to avoid bias and offer the option to notify them if a bias is encountered.
The user needs to be able to do legal research directly in the word-add-in in order to avoid switching between tools.
The user needs the settings to be easily discoverable in order to configure the tool to their needs.
The user would like to be supported in answering questions about their own document.
The user would like to be able to search through the chat to find information in older chats efficiently.
The user would like to be supported in prioritzing currently running workflows (and their results) to be able to know which one to focus on first.
The user would like to be supported in identifying who else needs to be involved in a specific case in a workflow to give them access to the case.
The user would like to be able to set company specific elements for workflows that can be shared and reused.
The user would like to have some guidance on how much information they need to provide to set up a workflow (step).
The user would like to be able to get an overview of which documents have been processed or involved in a workflow in order to check that nothing was missing.
The user expects the matrix analysis to show the citations from respective documents or sources.

quotes:
Wenn eine Matrix-Analyse durchgeführt wurde, sollte die Quelle für die extrahierte Antwort resp. die extrahierten Daten angezeigt werden. Die Kunden erwarten einen Hover-Effekt, in welchem ein Snippet aus der Original-Quelle angezeigt wird, damit die Daten schnell überprüft werden können.
Visitenkarten Bild habe ich nur hochgeladen, weil ich keinen Anhang zum hochladen habe (aber der Upload zwingend ist).

insight: Beck-Noxtua is showing an incorrect date in the chat

insight: The user wants the chat to be structured like legal case files, in order to fit their usual structures.

insight: Documents from DATEV DMS cannot be uploaded directly, they always get a file format error and have to save the file locally first as a workaround
quotes: Darüber hinaus ist uns aufgefallen, dass Dokumente aus DATEV DMS nicht direkt in Beck-Noxtua hochgeladen werden können. Hier erscheint immer wieder ein Fehler, dass das Dateiformat nicht zu verarbeiten sei. Wir müssen uns dann damit behelfen, dieses Dokument lokal abzuspeichern. Nur dann kann das Dokument nach Beck-Noxtua verschoben werden, damit es dann dort analysiert werden kann.

insight: Automatically generating document with employees names in Editor
The user expects the tool to not be biased by context that is given in a chat beforehand to stay objective.
The user needs the exact marginal numbers to be cited, so that the citations can be used directly in e.g. documents.
The user needs to be able to mark a section in the chat and prompt referring to this section in order to work more efficiently.
The user needs to be able to organize their (prompt) templates e.g. into folders to improve organization and finding specific templates more easily.

The user needs to be able to differentiate fine granularly what other users can do to templates/ workflows (i.e. creating, using, modifying, and deleting them), in order to have control over quality and access.
The user needs the sources to be linked (clickable) in the export and not only in the chat.
The user needs the tool to use smaller headlines for better readability of the export.
The user expect to be able to continue a chat even after a bit of time has passed.
The user needs the snippets of the citations to give them all context needed.
The user needs the chat overview to include when the chat happens.
If the prompt in a column is subsequently changed, the response is automatically updated, which is very important.
status: Well done - positive feedback outweighs negative

insight: The user needs clear transparency on existing limitations, e.g. upload limitations.
The user wishes the export of the matrix to keep the well-structured overview.
The users need the possibility to structure the data within the matrix in different ways.
status: Well done - positive feedback outweighs negative

insight: The users need a structured way to review multiple documents.
userGroup: Big Law Firm; Mixed; Corporate Law Department
status: Well done - positive feedback outweighs negative

insight: The user expects clear error messages, to be able to fix them, when uploads don't succeed in matrix analysis.
The user would like to be able to comment on matrixes in order to communicate with team members or other parties.
Users need to be able to add supporting documents to the Word-Add-In in order to adjust the document based on further info.
The user needs to be able to see and access old chats (within the Word-Add-In) in order to have access to all necessary information.
The User needs to be able to start a new chat (within the Word-Add-In) in order to start clean for a new topic.
The user needs the editor to save all made changes, when they change to another chat to avoid data loss.
The user needs the Word-Add-In to be able to work with footnotes in order to have sources less prominent in the document.
The user needs the tool to automatically select the right way of working in order to avoid having to do it manually.
The user needs the login to be smooth an easy and ideally not happen too often.

quotes: mitten im Chat abgebrochen, Timeouts / nur bei großen Dokumenten scheitert es manchmal
notes: Sentiment: Negative. Customer segment: Unknown (corporate/banking). Batch: feedback_batch_20260423.

insight: Users find that legal reasoning and subsumption in answers is too shallow, with alternative solutions, risk assessments, and minority views often missing.
quotes: Einzelne Antworten wirkten in der Herleitung zu kurz oder ohne alternative Lösungsansätze sowie ohne Risiken ausreichend darzustellen
notes: Sentiment: Negative. Customer segment: Unknown (corporate/banking). Batch: feedback_batch_20260423.

insight: Users cannot clearly tell whether the system is still processing or has already finished, due to missing or unclear progress indicators.
quotes: nicht immer klare Rückmeldung, ob ein Verarbeitungsvorgang noch läuft oder bereits abgeschlossen ist

insight: Multi-step reasoning is cut off after about 5 conversation turns; PDF upload size limits prevent processing larger documents.
quotes: es nicht möglich [ist], einen Gedanken mehrstufig zu entwickeln. Das System beendet die Kommunikation spätestens nach der 5. Stufe.

insight: Beck-Noxtua sales reports clients frequently ask for a chat-search feature because they don't name chats consistently and struggle to find prior conversations.
quotes: Beck-Noxtua Sales people are apparently often asked whether Noxtua will offer a Chat search function soon. They do not always name their chats properly and find it difficult to find information from previous chats.

insight: German-speaking clients find it odd that the Thinking Process is shown in English while answers are in German; they want the thought process in the prompt language.
quotes: see the AI thinking in German

insight: Saved Matrix Analysis prompts disappear when re-opening "Spalte bearbeiten"; users want Excel exports of a populated matrix to also include the prompts (separate sheet).
quotes: Es wäre außerdem sehr praktisch, wenn beim Excel-Export der befüllten Matrix auch die Prompts mit exportiert werden, gern auf einem separaten Tabellenblatt.

insight: Citation format for Swiss federal laws and rulings must follow Swiss conventions (e.g. "Art. 123 OR" not "OR - Art. 123"; BGer 5A_604/2024; BGE 148 III 161; plus Bundesstrafgericht/Bundesverwaltungsgericht/Bundespatentgericht each with own format).
quotes: Gesetze auf Bundesebene: Art. 123 OR (nicht OR - Art. 123)… Urteile auf Bundesebene: BGer 5A_604/2024, BGE 148 III 161… aus entscheidsuche API können zusätzlich Urteile des Bundesstrafgericht, Bundesverwaltungsgericht und Bundespatentgericht aggregiert werden, welche je eine eigene Zitierweise haben.

insight: User should be able to collapse the Thinking Process with one click after it is generated.
quotes: Es sollte mit einem Klick möglich sein den Denkprozess einzuklappen, nachdem dieser generiert wurde.

insight: Noxtua should send structured KPI/log data to HLV (TTFB, response time, conversation table, error stats, click tracking on legalis links, cited_sources) for performance monitoring and author royalty calculations - via API/webhook.
quotes: Noxtua soll Log-Daten strukturiert an HLV übermitteln zur Messung von Performance, Nutzerverhalten und Content-Nutzung für Autorenhonorar-Berechnung.

insight: Until point-in-time legal queries are supported, older editions should be ranked lower than newer ones; also: support delete commands per bucket; avoid daily re-indexing of unchanged laws.
quotes: Solange keine Point-In-Time-Betrachtung des Rechts vorgenommen werden kann, sollten Altauflagen weniger relevant eingestuft werden, als neuere.

insight: Integrate cantonal jurisprudence and laws (entscheidsuche API + cantonal legal databases) into the knowledge base.
quotes: Integration der Daten aus dem Scaping der entscheidsuche API und der kantonalen Gesetzesdatenbanken. V1 soll gemäss heutiger Besprechung bereits nächste Woche auf Dev deployed werden.

insight: Model must reflect the hierarchy of Swiss legal sources (federal vs cantonal precedence) and the differentiated binding effect of court decisions when ranking and answering.
quotes: Die hierarchische Struktur der Rechtsordnung mit Vorrangregeln (Bundesrecht vor kantonalem Recht) und deren Ausnahmen… präzise abbilden und bei der Relevanzbewertung von Quellen berücksichtigen. Das System muss die differenzierte Bindungswirkung von Gerichtsentscheiden (Präjudizien… vs. präjudizielle Erwägungen) erkennen und bei der Antwortgenerierung entsprechend gewichten.

insight: Use the legalis "Verweise" cross-reference structure to populate the Knowledge Graph and improve citation quality / document interconnection.
quotes: Prüfung, ob die bestehende Verweisstruktur von legalis (Reiter „Verweise

insight: Multilingual source matching: French-language questions should also surface German/Italian/English references (and reverse).
quotes: Bei französischsprachigen Fragestellungen sollten auch deutsche / italienische / englische Referenzen berücksichtigt werden (und umgekehrt).

insight: Broaden source coverage so multiple commentaries (BSK StGB/JStG, CR CP II, PraxKomm StGB, HK EMRK, etc.) are surfaced rather than a single source per topic.
Source filtering / discovery still misses specific known works and rulings (e.g. BGer 6S.62/2000); filter on works/categories/rulings should be reliable.
quotes: Generell stelle ich fest, dass bestimmte Werke inzwischen schon viel besser gefunden werden. Dennoch habe ich folgende Beispiele für nicht gefundene Quellen bzw. nicht funktionierenden Quellen-Filtern. Prompt: BGer 6S.62/2000 vom 30. September 1998 zusammenfassen. Antwort: Leider konnte ich den spezifischen Bundesgerichtsentscheid… in der verfügbaren Datenbank nicht finden.

insight: Right-pane / hover-box source previews currently show the start of the snippet - they should show the exact passage that proves the cited statement.
quotes: Aktuell zeigt die Vorschau schlichtweg den Beginn des Snippets an, besser wäre es wenn genau diejenige Stelle aus dem Werk zitiert würde, welche durch die Quelle bewiesen werden soll.

insight: Status request for the previously discussed "3 model-suggested follow-up questions" feature; if shipped, elisa needs them via API as well.
quotes: Ihr hattet mal erwähnt, dass ihr euch auch für den Workspace überlegt 3 vom Model vorgeschlagene Folgefragen zu generieren. Was ist der Status bezüglich diesem Vorhaben? Falls dies umgesetzt würde, bräuchten wir auch für elisa die entsprechenden Folgefragen über die API response.

insight: Footnote handling in source previews: full snippet with footnotes is hard to read; need a smarter way to display footnote-rich passages.
quotes: In der Vorschau in der Right-Pane und in der Hover-Box wird derzeit der ganze Snippet angezeigt. Bei Fussnoten führt dies zu einem schwer lesbaren Text mit unschönen Zeilenumbrüchen. Das Dilema ist, dass die Fussnoten durchaus teilweise sehr relevanten Text… enthalten können.

insight: Tool-call display in elisa's Thinking Process currently shows raw API function names (e.g. "german_law_lookup"); needs human-readable labels.
quotes: Wir arbeiten gerade an der Darstellung der Tool Calls im Thinking Process von elisa. Aktuell zeigen wir die technischen Function Names aus eurer API (z.B. „german_law_lookup

insight: Chats should be persisted server-side rather than in the browser.
quotes:
Mir ist bewusst, dass dies bereits in Umsetzung ist. Das Ticket dient nur dem Status-Tracking.
"Maybe a desktop application alongside, with browser in background" (Corporate Personas)

insight: Thinking Process should always be in the language of the prompt.
quotes: Ich weiss, dass ihr daran bereits arbeitet. Das Ticket dient nur dem Status-Tracking.

insight: Support templates for contracts and legal pleadings.
When uploaded documents are queried, the answer should reference the specific text passages used; in long documents users currently have to search manually for the cited paragraph.
quotes: Wir der Dokumentupload genutzt und diesbezügliche Fragen beantwortet, wäre ein passender Verweis auf Textpassagen des Dokuments wünschenswert. Gerade bei sehr umfassenden Dokumenten muss man sehr lange suchen um jenen Absatz zu identifizieren, welcher vom System zur Beantwortung verwendet wurde.

insight: Show the document date directly with each source - useful indicator of how current/outdated the content is.
quotes: Das Dokumentdatum ist ein hilfreiches Indiz, ob ein Inhalt noch aktuell oder eher veraltet ist. Es wäre gut, dies direkt bei der Quellenanzeige darzustellen.

insight: Court-level filter: when a question targets a specific court (e.g. OGH), only that court should be searched. Currently other courts are pulled in.
quotes: Fragen zu bestimmten Gerichten sollten auch nur jenes Gericht durchsuchen. Im angehängten Export werden mehrere Gerichte herangezogen obwohl nur nach dem OGH gefragt war.

insight: After switching from legalis to entscheidsuche API for Bundesgerichtsentscheide, the citation format is no longer correct - needs adjustment for cantonal rulings index.
quotes: Bundesgerichtsentscheide werden neu aus der entscheidsuche API bezogen und nicht mehr aus legalis. Dies führt dazu, dass die Zitierweise nun nicht mehr korrekt ist.

insight: Avoid listing the exact same source multiple times in the same paragraph.
Follow-up questions are answered without taking the prior question's context (e.g. the previously named statute) into account - context is not carried forward.

LLM produces hallucinated, misleading or legally incorrect explanations across many areas of Swiss law (employment, criminal, family, contract, data protection) and fabricates German court citations (e.g. BGH); even when the conclusion is correct, the reasoning is often flawed. Consolidates 21 systematic Swiss-law QA test reports plus 1 Beck-Noxtua attorney report.
source: Publisher Feedback - Swiss; Publisher Feedback - Beck Germany
quotes: Grds. sind wir von der KI angetan. Man bekommt im Researchbereich Antworten, die juristisch auf viel hoeherem Niveau als bei Konkurrenten sein duerften. ... Teilweise fantasieren die Antworten aber ziemlich.

insight: AI should detect deadlines/urgency from incoming items (e.g. BeA messages, emails) and proactively create calendar entries, tasks and reminders
quotes: "If I get a BeA message, it should immediately flag: attention, deadlines apply, and propose one - like the iPhone does | Estimate how fast it must be done, put a task in the calendar, remind me if I don't process it fast enough" (Corporate Personas)

insight: AI agents/self-service surfaces per business unit so non-legal users can self-serve, with legal curating the knowledge base and human-in-the-loop escalation when needed
quotes: "Business unit sees the UI; I curate the database | Agents for each business unit... they recognize when human-in-the-loop is needed" (Corporate Personas)

insight: AI helps decode unfamiliar business-unit jargon to clarify what the case is even about
quotes: "Pasting it in, it figured out 'this is banking regulation context' - that helped illuminate the facts" (Corporate Personas)

insight: AI should ingest requests from multiple input channels (email, intranet form, voice/phone) into one structured intake
quotes: "A tool with different input options - email, intranet integration, maybe voice so I can call in my problem and attach documents" (Corporate Personas)

insight: AI should balance workload across team members based on current capacity
quotes: "Shift things between employees based on workload - if peaks are at one colleague, push to someone else" (Corporate Personas)

insight: AI should keep requesters informed of progress automatically
quotes: "Keep the requester informed about individual steps in between" (Corporate Personas)

insight: Gamification elements (streaks, daily nudges) would motivate consistent tool use
quotes: "What's good about Duolingo is the gamification - puts pressure but doesn't take much time per day" (Corporate Personas)

insight: Want comparative drafting where AI redlines existing Word doc minimally rather than regenerating a new draft
quotes: "Lay it against in a Word file, not get a complete new draft but only slightly adjust individual sentences" (Corporate Personas)

insight: Need AI to flag cross-clause and cross-domain (tax, antitrust) impacts when one clause changes
quotes: "If you change something here, ping me that it could have a tax or antitrust impact - don't just edit this one clause" (Corporate Personas)

insight: Want to generate visual diagrams (Schaubilder) of contractual relationships from large contracts
quotes: "Upload a 500-page contract and say: make me a diagram of the contractual relationships, who delivers what to whom" (Corporate Personas)

insight: Need a tabular overview of contractual obligations, rights, and termination notice periods extracted from a contract
quotes: "An overview: notice periods, my rights, my duties, what happens when a contract ends - neatly in a table" (Corporate Personas)

insight: Need AI to optimize my own English drafting on demand ('make this proper legal English')
quotes: "We work a lot with English contracts - 'make legal English of this' to optimize phrasing is really useful" (Corporate Personas)

insight: Want AI to cover EU regulations and EU case law alongside national law in one tool
quotes: "Not only national law but also overarching European law in one AI tool, including the related rulings" (Corporate Personas)

insight: Need unlimited input/output length for template-filling tasks
quotes: "20-page template with 400 blanks to be filled - want arbitrary input/output" (Corporate Personas)

insight: Vendor proactively curates templates/checklists in tool over time
quotes: "Functionality fills up - 'they made a template for this, I can use it directly'" (Corporate Personas)

insight: AI should auto-suggest relevant case law/judgments in the background when opening a case file
quotes: "When I open my case file... prompts have already been sent that show me important judgments that could belong to it" (Judiciary Personas)

insight: AI should auto-extract procedural data (admissibility, deadlines, time limits) from case files immediately
quotes: "I open the file, it tells me directly: here is the deadline information, and: this is already time-barred" (Judiciary Personas)

insight: Need cross-border procedural comparison (e.g. covert seizure in Austria) for trans-national cases
quotes: "Can they do a covert seizure in Austria? Today this is always laborious research" (Judiciary Personas)

insight: Need AI to remind me what I already read/wrote in a long-running case so I don't redo work
quotes: "You forget what you already read when you're working in pieces - you do the work twice or three times" (Judiciary Personas)

insight: Need AI to flag contradictions between submissions and suggest fact-finding gaps for offence elements
quotes: "Highlight contradictions between submissions, prompt fact-finding suggestions for missing elements of the offence" (Judiciary Personas)

insight: Need AI to verify and link cited rulings/sources within the parties' incoming submissions
quotes: "Linking of judgements that are cited in submissions, checking citations" (Judiciary Personas)

insight: AI should be able to make tool calls / autonomous internet research instead of needing manual context
quotes: "Ability to use tools, especially to do internet research itself" (Judiciary Personas)

insight: Want agentic automation of administrative steps (e.g. dispatch decree to attorneys whose addresses are known)
quotes: "I write a decree, it could be recognized automatically... addresses are in the system, why does a person still do it manually?" (Judiciary Personas)

insight: Want inline AI clause suggestions in Word (Copilot-style) while drafting a contract
quotes: "A Copilot-like sidebar in Word with Noxtua suggesting clause variants 1, 2, 3, 4 as I draft would be a huge relief" (Corporate Personas)
